topics: The common misconception that Roman gladiators always fought to the death based on archaeological evidence
The historical inaccuracy of the 'Dark Ages' label for early medieval Europe, exemplified by the intellectual flourishing in centers like the scriptorium of Tours under Alcuin (late 8th century)
The Papal Bull 'Inter Caetera' (1493) and the subsequent Treaty of Tordesillas (1494) as foundational decisions for global territorial division between Spain and Portugal.
Correcting the popular narrative that the 1983 Able Archer exercise nearly triggered nuclear war, based on declassified intelligence assessments from both sides.
